project.name: NKRYPT

plaque.haiku:
Mystery beyond
Decipher the veiled meaning
Renown awaits you
plaque.key_phrase: From simple to hard, the key to the last is found in all the rest.
plaque.centenary_note: One code within NKRYPT celebrates Canberra's Centenary. Be first to decipher it and win a great prize.
plaque.supporters: Mr Eddie Kutner and Mr Leon Kemplar

project.meta_pattern: Every solved plaintext describes its own cipher's history or technique (self-referential).

naming.note: Two naming schemes exist. meme.net.au uses letters A-H by fiducial spacing. dkrypt.org uses numbers 1-8.

ciphers.name: Geospatial
ciphers.type: Number string as lat/lon GPS coordinates
ciphers.note: Solution to Questacon's Centenary Code challenge (2014)

ciphers.name: Caesar/shift/al-Kindi/Vigenere
ciphers.type: Progressive shift ciphers (Caesar shift 1, shift 2, al-Kindi frequency, Vigenere)
ciphers.ciphertext:
A LETTER SHIFT A CIPHER MAKES
A FAMOUS ROMANS NAME IT TAKES

B MFUUFS TIJGU B DJQIFS NBLFT
B GBNPVT SPNBOT OBNF JU UBLFT

C UJKHV QH VYQ AQW PQY ECP DTGCM
DWV QVJGT OQXGU C EQFG ECP OCMG

KRZHYHU WKHB EH VZDSSHG DERXW
D NXID PDQ FRXOG ILQG WKHP RXW

RPKR QMWJZZKRG WYMABY
INGY PZBZIE XROZA
OPK RNQV SA ENMPL
XVZIZ GBHV ERIQIF
ciphers.plaintext:
A letter shift a cipher makes
 a famous Roman's name it takes [Caesar]
A shift of two you now can break
 but other moves a code can make [shift]
However they be swapped about
 a Kufa man could find them out [al-Kindi]
When different shifts
 each letter takes
The name of which
 great code awakes [Vigenere]

ciphers.name: Semaphore (Chappe)
ciphers.plaintext_summary: Poem about French optical telegraph ("code that flowed from gay Paris...")
ciphers.plaintext:
Code that flowed from gay Paris
 a message plain for all to see
 now demands a stronger key
Improved by time in dribs and drabs
 three prime examples in dark crabs
ciphers.description: Chappe semaphore system. Each symbol transcribed as a number pair representing arm angles (45, 90, 135, 225, 270, 315 degrees).

ciphers.name: Scytale
ciphers.ciphertext:
AGPSHALHALIECNREWTOCTPUWS
MEOLOLOADOEEEOSAAHVHEERIP
EHNAWLSVMFNCRFICCAESRDENA
SIHVNBEEIATETAAEOTDMHEHFR
SDIETYRARNGUAPNSDPMAENIOT
AUSSOCSNACRNIEPAERURLSSRA
ciphers.plaintext_summary: Poem about Histiaeus and Lysander of Sparta
ciphers.plaintext:
A message hid upon his slave
 shown to all by closer shave
An admiral of ancient Greece
 uncertain of a Persian peace
Saw a code that proved much smarter
 helped ensure his win for Sparta
ciphers.description: Scytale wrapped around a hexagonal baton. References Histiaeus (message tattooed on slave's head) and Lysander of Sparta (scytale message, 404 BC).

ciphers.name: Binary
ciphers.plaintext_summary: Names of telegraph/wireless pioneers (Branly, Braun, Cooke, Faraday, Gauss, Henry, Hertz, Marconi, Morse, etc.)
ciphers.plaintext: Branly, Braun, Campillo, Cooke, DeMoura, Dyar, Faraday, Gauss, Henry, Hertz, Marconi, Morse, Rutherford, Schilling, Soemmering, Sturgeon, Tesla, Vail, Weber, Wheatstone

ciphers.name: Rotor
ciphers.ciphertext_line_1: BIOB AXQC NLPA MNXE SBNT FJLD DL
ciphers.ciphertext_line_2: JAWS FDHD MATX EJHM PVUJ XJOM KH
ciphers.attempts_summary: Single-key 26^4 brute force, 12+ rotor advancement schemes, L1/L2 group pairings, beam search, traversal codes as keys - all rejected.
ciphers.note: Cannot be cracked from pillar data alone. Needs cross-pillar information.
ciphers.ciphertext:
BIOB AXQC NLPA MNXE SBNT FJLD DL
JAWS FDHD MATX EJHM PVUJ XJOM KH

ciphers.name: Pigpen
ciphers.type: Pigpen cipher, German text, RTL lines upside-down
ciphers.plaintext_summary: Queen of the Night aria from Mozart's Die Zauberfloete, ending "123"
ciphers.note: Pigpen = Freemason cipher; Magic Flute has Masonic motifs; "123" may reference the three opening chords (Eb minor, C minor, Eb major), three Masonic pillars, or three veiled ladies
ciphers.plaintext:
Zum Leiden bin ich auserkoren
 Denn meine Tochter fehlet mir;
Durch sie ging all mein Gluck verloren
 Ein Bosewicht entfloh mit ihr
Noch seh ich sie zittern.
 1 2 3

(I am chosen for suffering,
 for my daughter is gone from me;
Through her all my happiness has been lost,
 a villain fled with her
I can still see her trembling. 1 2 3)
ciphers.description: Pigpen cipher in German. The plaintext is the Queen of the Night aria from Mozart's Die Zauberfloete. Pigpen was commonly used by the Freemasons, and the opera contains rationalist Freemason motifs.

ciphers.name: Cogs
ciphers.attempts_summary: Direct substitution, angle mapping, Polybius, Vigenere/Beaufort with 80+ keys, Friedman analysis - all rejected. Multi-channel encoding likely.

mesh_groups.pattern: A | BCD | E | F | G | HIJK | L | MNO | P
AB | C | D | E | FG | H | I
ABCD | EF | G | H | I | J
A | B | C | D | E | FG | HI

ciphers.description: 71 cog-shaped glyphs across 7 columns. Each cog has 2-4 sets of teeth, a dot angle, and internal dots. Adjacent cogs in a column either mesh (teeth interleave) or clash. 43 mesh-compatibility groups identified.

ciphers.name: Braille
ciphers.plaintext_summary: Poem about Napoleon, Barbier, and Braille
ciphers.plaintext:
A short-sighted general with buses to park [Napoleon]
 an old French Barber sitting quite in the dark [Barbier]
Tip of a finger, glide of a snail [Braille]
 all our great works on the head of a nail.
ciphers.description: Braille cipher with right-to-left lines upside-down. References Napoleon, Barbier (night writing), and Louis Braille.

ciphers.name: Rail fence
ciphers.plaintext_summary: Poem about Alberti, Trithemius, and Rossignol
ciphers.plaintext:
A simple code to hide your tails
 it's letters nailed to different rails [rail fence cipher]
A famous scholar from times past
 with copper discs encoded fast [Alberti]
A German monk with codes so strong
 used keys to shift the cipher on [Trithemius]
A family down the mountainside
 a cabinet noir they worked inside [Rossignol]

ciphers.name: DNA codons
ciphers.type: DNA 3-letter codons mapped to amino acid single-letter codes
ciphers.plaintext: Far sighted lady, we may spawn a repainted thylacine
ciphers.note: May reference conservation geneticist Karen Firestone and thylacine DNA recovery
ciphers.ciphertext: TTC GCA CGA TCG ATC GGT CAT ACG GAG GAC CTG GCA GAC TAC TGG GAG ATG GCA TAC TCG CCG GCA TGG AAC GCA CGA GAG CCG GCA ATC AAC ACG GAG GAC ACG CAT TAC CTG GCA TGC ATC AAC GAG
ciphers.description: DNA 3-letter codons mapped to amino acid single-letter codes. May reference conservation geneticist Karen Firestone and the Australian Museum thylacine DNA recovery project.

ciphers.name: Astroids
ciphers.type: Four-pointed star glyphs (U+2726) in 42 columns with irregular vertical spacing
ciphers.conjecture: May represent light spectra (stars or elements)
ciphers.attempts_summary: Count-to-letter, Polybius pairs, y-position mapping, binary grid, spectral matching (H, He, Li, Na etc.), music/constellation mapping - all rejected. Likely needs cross-pillar key from paired pillar G.

ciphers.name: Squircles
ciphers.creator_hint: Vertical pairs form 26x5 labyrinth grid; 4 squircles per plaintext letter (codon-like); approximately 65 letters of plaintext
ciphers.best_reading: Pair-boustrophedon odd start gives 50 distinct 4-cell codons; pattern (1,3,0,3) appears 8 times (12.3% matches English E)
ciphers.attempts_summary: All simple substitutions, Vigenere, Bifid, Hill, autokey, running-key with solved poems, SA on homophonic - all rejected. 44 of 65 positions are singletons = too many free variables for frequency attack.
ciphers.description: 10x26 grid of 4-orientation petal/cam shapes (encoded 0-3). Creator hint (Kohlhagen via Schmeh): vertical pairs form a 26x5 labyrinth grid; 4 squircles per plaintext letter (codon-like redundancy); approximately 65 letters of plaintext. Best reading config: pair-boustrophedon with odd start column gives 50 distinct 4-cell codons. Pattern (1,3,0,3) appears 8 times (12.3%), matching English E frequency (12.7%).

ciphers.note: IC flat; definitively not simple substitution. Lines offset like a double-helix projection; sine wave freq approximately 0.75 rad/line
ciphers.description: 515 characters in double-helix visual layout. Lines offset like a plane projection of a double helix. Sine wave frequency approximately 0.75 radians per line, with line endings lagging by a quarter circle. IC = 1.014 (flat); chi-squared vs English = 2205. Definitively not simple substitution or transposition.

ciphers.name: Labyrinth
ciphers.plaintext:
A labyrinth stands before you
 and is but one of eight
Mark all the twists and turns
 for patterns they create
That one by one step through
 the enigmas that await

ciphers.note: Likely requires combined keys from all other ciphers. Frequency distribution is flat (not simple substitution).

ring_ciphers.description: All 8 pillars carry a 26x5 letter/symbol grid at the same height (1709mm). Read in labyrinth (boustrophedon) pattern.
ring_ciphers.traversal_pattern: Row 1 LTR, Row 2 RTL, Row 3 LTR, Row 4 RTL, Row 5 LTR. One down and one up transition between each row pair.

pairings.insight: Ring ciphers at uniform 1709mm height. Pillar height variation = extension above ring. Lower ciphers cluster into pairs by effective extent.

pairs.relationship: Tightest pair, same group on both upper and lower channels
pairs.attack: A's Caesar/geospatial solutions may key C's rotor
B's semaphore/scytale solutions feed H's labyrinth/PVL
F's DNA solution may key G's waveform
E's railfence/braille solutions may shift D's cogs decoding

constellation.hypothesis: Pillars B,G,F,C,D form the Southern Cross (Crux). Pillars A,E,H are Alpha/Beta/Omega Centauri.
constellation.thematic_basis: Centaurus constellation adjacent to Crux; 'Centenary'/'Centaurus' wordplay for Canberra's 1913-2013 centenary.
constellation.geometric_note: Procrustes fit best subset is {C,E,F,G,H} at norm 0.449, but BGFCD chosen on thematic grounds.

base_code.description: 15 autosomal STR loci per pillar base, matching PowerPlex 16 HS forensic DNA profiling.

partial_solution.note: SWYQ = ROT13(FJLD), where FJLD is the 6th group of rotor ciphertext line 1

rotor_machine.description: Four movable rotors plus fixed reflector, analogous to Enigma but with manual advancement.
rotor_machine.advancement: Manual (encoder chooses stepping sequence; no automatic odometer)
